column4: Comment
Kayos absolutely slapped! The energy was insane from the first act.
Samay roasting Mukul Vinja getting a 3.6 average after rating himself 8.3 was peak comedy 😭
DK got 9.5 from judges AND rated himself 9.5. That self-awareness deserves a standing ovation.
Rakhi Sawant as a judge was chaotic but honestly perfect casting for the first episode.
The Twins getting 9.6 average in Episode 1 is insane. They were genuinely elite.
Vedant Agarwal rated himself 7.9 and got a 0 from judges. Samay's reaction was gold.
Simran & Siddhant were the hidden gems of Ep1. 9.3 average and deserved every bit.
Maheep Singh and Rakhi arguing was already better TV than anything on mainstream channels.
Decibel Crew getting 9.3 was justified. Best group performance of the episode easily.
Jyoti Sharma's 6.5 from judges vs her 6 self-score — she was perfectly self-aware lol.
Aashish Solanki as guest judge was great. Kept it real without being unnecessarily harsh.
Samay's hosting in Ep1 was just effortless. You can tell this is HIS show.
Sandeepan Ganguly giving himself an 8.5 but getting 7.1 is painfully accurate to most talent shows.
Yashraj Singh Talwar's energy as a judge added so much to the panel banter.
Vedant Modi being perfectly accurate at 7.8 is weirdly satisfying.
The format of letting contestants score themselves is genuinely the best reality TV innovation in years.
Rakhi Sawant throwing shade every two minutes made Ep1 the most chaotic opener possible — loved it.
Kayos is criminally underrated. Hope they got signed after this.
Decibel Crew and DK in the same episode? Ep1 carried hard.
Samay's deadpan reaction to Vedant getting a 0 is the most I've laughed at a reality show.
Harshini getting a PERFECT 10 from judges was completely deserved. Absolutely stunning act.
Harsh Kala rating himself 10 and getting 9.5 — the AUDACITY but also somehow justified 😭
Gaurav rating himself 0 and still getting a 7 from judges is the most wholesome thing in the show's history.
Bharti Singh on the judge panel was electric. She never lets the energy drop.
Prem giving himself a 2 and getting 3.5... I mean at least he was self-aware lol.
Haarsh Limbachiyaa and Bharti together on the panel = free entertainment. Chemistry is insane.
Sarvesh's act was genuinely impressive. 8 from judges feels slightly low actually.
Tony Kakkar as a judge? That's a choice. At least he was funnier here than his songs 😂
Suryansh & Veer getting a clean 8 both from themselves and judges — true professionals.
Samay absolutely roasted Tony Kakkar's scoring consistency and it was hilarious.
Anant Upadhyay's 40-minute slot? That man deserved a whole episode by himself.
Harsh Kala is the kind of energy every show needs. That man is a star.
Bharti being genuinely sweet to contestants while still being brutally honest is rare judge energy.
Gaurav showing up with a 0 self-score and somehow charming the panel is peak India's Got Latent.
Tony Kakkar's judging style felt a bit out of place among the comedians. But at least he tried.
Harshini deserves a Netflix special. That performance was not supposed to happen on a YouTube show.
Samay clowning on Prem for the 2/3.5 gap was comedy gold.
Haarsh Limbachiyaa surprised me positively as a judge. Balanced and funny.
Anant Upadhyay getting 7.5 for a 40-minute performance is lowkey disrespectful 😂
Episode 2 had the best overall contestant quality of the early episodes. That Harshini moment was TV history.
Pranav & Advaya getting 9.5 — the best duo of Episode 3 without question.
Raghu Ram back in a judge's chair felt nostalgic. He was sharp and fair.
Devpreet Bhavsar gave himself 8.5 and got 6.5. That self-confidence gap is very relatable.
Tanmay Bhat on the panel is always a win. His takes are so sharp.
Suryansh Tiwari getting exactly 8 from both himself and judges — balanced king.
Dr. Sid Warrier brought such a different energy to the judge panel. Refreshing choice.
Saubhagya Jindal giving 8.5 and getting 6 is the most classic overestimation this show has seen.
Tanmay and Raghu arguing over a score mid-episode was more entertaining than half the acts.
Deepak Yadav was perfectly accurate at 7/7. Respectable and grounded.
Ritik Kadam at 7.5 self-score and 6 from judges — gap wasn't huge but the judges were harsh.
Samay's chemistry with Tanmay Bhat is unbeatable. These two just elevate each other.
Pranav & Advaya were robbed of a 10. That act was flawless.
Raghu Ram being the 'serious one' on the panel while everyone clowns around is hilarious contrast.
Devpreet's confidence before the act vs. after the score reveal was heartbreaking and funny simultaneously.
Dr. Sid Warrier gave the most medically precise feedback I've ever heard on a comedy show 😂
Ep3 had the weakest average scores overall but Pranav & Advaya saved it completely.
Tanmay Bhat's roast of Saubhagya's self-score was BRUTAL but earned.
Suryansh Tiwari is the kind of contestant who makes this format work. No ego, pure talent.
Samay keeping score disputes hilarious without letting them get personal is a masterclass in hosting.
Episode 3 felt slower but Tanmay and Raghu on the same panel made it binge-worthy.
Bacha (Rohit) getting 9.5 from the judges was absolutely earned. What a performance.
Deepak Kalal as a judge is just chaos in human form and I am HERE for it.
Puneet Malhotra giving himself 9.5 and getting a 2 is the single biggest gap in the show's history.
Hard Shivam deserved that 9.0. The judges were right to go high.
Manan Desai was a surprisingly thoughtful judge. Did not expect that from this panel.
Kiran Holkar scoring 10 self and 5 from judges — bold but very off 😂
Samay's reaction to Puneet Malhotra's 2 average score was peak silent comedy.
Deepak Kalal and Manan Desai together on a judge panel is something no one asked for but we needed.
Shivani Rai giving herself 9 and getting 8.5 — almost perfect self-assessment. Queen behaviour.
Shahid Khan got only 5 from judges after giving himself an 8. There's a story there.
Bharat Ka Oscar is the best contestant name in the show's history, no contest.
Deepak Kalal scoring randomly with no logic was both frustrating and chaotically entertaining.
Bacha (Rohit) should be signed immediately. 9.5 average speaks for itself.
Agu Stanley on the panel was a great pick — international perspective, funny energy.
Hard Shivam clearly rehearsed a lot. You could tell from the precision. Deserved the 9.
Manan Desai seemed underprepared to handle some of the acts. Needed more personality.
Puneet Malhotra getting a 2 while looking genuinely surprised is the funniest moment in the season.
Kiran Holkar was delusional but in the most endearing way. Love that energy honestly.
Episode 4 had the biggest score gaps. Samay milked every moment perfectly.
Shivani Rai was the most consistent performer of Ep4. Judges were fair with her.
Sourav Vaishnav getting 8.5 from judges is peak deserved. That skill was ridiculous.
Poonam Pandey as a judge was... an experience. Certainly unpredictable scoring.
Utkarsh Jhole & Sanjana Chatwani getting 5.5 after rating 9 is such a classic Latent gap moment.
Shweta Singh's act translating to an 8 average was solid. Judges were aligned.
Vidit Gujrathi — a chess grandmaster judging performers is the most India's Got Latent thing possible.
Nikhil Ing getting 6 from judges after an 8.5 self-score. The gap was painful but accurate.
Samay trolling Poonam Pandey's scoring rationale in real time was glorious.
Vidit Gujrathi calculating scores like a chess position was both accurate and hilarious.
Shweta Singh showed real stage presence. The 8 was fair.
Sagar Shah and Vidit together is basically ChessBase India on a talent show lol.
Utkarsh and Sanjana's confidence vs reality check was the most dramatic scoring of Ep5.
Poonam Pandey scoring based on vibes alone while Vidit scored algorithmically is peak panel chaos.
Sourav Vaishnav is the dark horse of the series. That 8.5 should have been a 9.
Sagar Shah brings a very grounded energy to the panel. Good balance with the chaotic guests.
Nikhil Ing was good but slightly overconfident. The judges were honest.
Samay's hosting in Ep5 was especially tight. He balanced the chaos really well.
Poonam Pandey's presence made judges' deliberations unpredictable — which was actually entertaining.
Shweta Singh coming in calm and delivering was the most pleasant surprise of the episode.
Vidit Gujrathi is officially my favourite guest judge of the whole season. Precision king.
Ep5 felt shorter than usual but Sourav and Shweta carried it well.
Sharon Verma getting 9.5 from the judges! That opening act set the bar impossibly high.
P. Santosh Patra's act was divisive but the 9 average says the judges loved it.
The judges endorsing derogatory remarks during Santosh Patra's segment was genuinely disturbing.
Sharvari Bandodkar gave herself a 6 and got a 7 — she undersold herself!
Ravi Gupta as a judge felt underutilised in Ep6 but was sharp when he spoke.
Sevarshi Barua getting a clean 8.5 both ways. Solid self-awareness and solid act.
Ansh Das finishing with 9.5 was THE highlight of Episode 6. What a closer.
Comic Saurabh on the panel was a pleasant surprise — he balanced funny and fair really well.
The Santosh Patra controversy soured an otherwise strong episode. Judges should have stepped in.
Rahgir Live as a judge was a fresh and underrated pick. Brought street cred.
Ansh Das carried Ep6 on his back. 9.5 is conservative — should've been a 10.
Sharon Verma being the first act AND getting 9.5 is bold. Delivered completely.
Comic Saurabh roasting other judges mid-judging was meta and hilarious.
Sharvari Bandodkar's self-score of 6 vs judges' 7 shows the most humble contestant of the season.
Ravi Gupta seemed to take scoring seriously but needed more personality to pop on screen.
P. Santosh Patra's act was funny in delivery but the surrounding discourse was awful.
Sevarshi Barua is the most consistent mid-episode contestant. Reliable and watchable.
The judges' panel in Ep6 was actually well-balanced when not being problematic.
Samay handled some of the Ep6 tension relatively well. But more intervention was needed.
Ansh Das and Sharon Verma are the twin pillars of Episode 6. Both at 9.5. Elite.
Parneet Singh getting a perfect 9 both self and judges. Absolute pro.
Vipul Goyal on the panel is always a good time. Dry humour fits the show perfectly.
Manmeet Singh & Karan closing Ep7 with a 9 average? Great way to end.
Ayush Sharma getting 8.5 from judges in the opening slot was impressive.
Nishant Tanwar / Joke Singh was surprisingly sharp as a judge. Held his own.
Toshika Bisht perfectly matched her self-score of 8.5. Clean and accurate.
Vishesh Rai giving 8.5 and getting 6 — ouch. Big miss on self-assessment.
Sonali Thakker as a judge added a very different and welcome perspective to the panel.
Vipul Goyal effortlessly clowning between acts while still being constructive = ideal judge.
Parneet Singh's act in Ep7 was criminally underrated in online discourse.
Samay and Vipul Goyal's banter in Ep7 could carry a whole podcast.
Vishesh Rai was a decent act but the 6 average told the story — judges weren't convinced.
Joke Singh's comedic scoring commentary was a bonus layer of entertainment in Ep7.
Manmeet & Karan closing with a 9 was the most satisfying ending of any episode so far.
Toshika Bisht is quietly one of the most self-aware and consistent contestants of the season.
Sonali Thakker balanced fairness and humour really well for a first-time appearance.
Ayush Sharma starting the episode strong set the tone perfectly. Great opener.
Nishant Tanwar's roast of a low-scoring contestant mid-show had the audience on the floor.
Samay knew exactly when to let the panel speak and when to cut in. Masterclass in Ep7.
Episode 7 might be the most balanced episode in terms of act quality AND judge panel.
Viral Mistry starting with 9.5 from judges is a statement. Ep8 began STRONG.
Kunal Kamra as a judge is everything you'd expect — surgical, blunt, and hilarious.
Rajesh Yadav also getting 9.5 like Viral Mistry? Ep8 judges were in a generous mood.
Amber Sharma getting a 9 after his 8.5 self-score — slight overperformer and we respect it.
Atul Khatri's scoring was thoughtful and his jokes between acts were gold.
Madhura Joshi getting only 5.5 from judges after an 8.5 self-score — tough crowd in Ep8.
Kunal Kamra roasting a contestant mid-act without pausing was peak unfiltered comedy.
Raksha Tomar gave herself 6.5 and got 6. At least she knew exactly where she stood.
Praveen Bhairwa getting a clean 9/9 — another episode of someone correctly reading their own skill level.
Kunal Kamra and Samay's energy together was electric. More of this please.
Atul Khatri maintaining humour while still being genuinely evaluative was impressive.
Viral Mistry and Rajesh Yadav both hitting 9.5 back to back made Ep8 the highest-scoring episode.
Madhura Joshi getting 5.5 was hard to watch. The judges were probably fair but it stung.
Praveen Bhairwa closing Ep8 with a 9/9 match was a chef's kiss ending.
Amber Sharma slightly overperforming his self-score is such a feel-good moment.
Kunal Kamra not being afraid to give very low scores with very precise reasoning was refreshing.
Raksha Tomar showing humility with a 6.5 self-score is rare on this show.
Atul Khatri is one of the most underrated judge picks of the whole season.
Samay keeping Ep8 tight despite two high-scoring consecutive acts is proper show management.
Episode 8 is statistically the strongest episode of the season. Two 9.5s and a 9/9 match.
Rishikesh Yadav & MC Azad getting a perfect 10 from judges is the highlight of the entire series.
Maheep Singh back for Ep9 — his chemistry with Samay is always hilarious.
Bhavya getting 9.5 from judges after a 9 self-score. Slight underestimation and it paid off!
Aashish Solanki in the guest judge chair again. His consistency is why he keeps being invited.
Yash Sharma giving 7.5 and getting 6 — it happens. Judges seemed unmoved.
Amit Tandon brought a mature energy to judging in Ep9. His feedback was actually constructive.
Rishikesh & MC Azad getting a 10 while Samay sat completely speechless = legendary TV moment.
MC Azad's performance alone could carry a Spotify playlist. That 10 was non-negotiable.
Aashish Solanki is the most consistent recurring guest judge on the show. Always fair, always funny.
Bhavya was a great discovery from Ep9. That 9.5 was hard-earned.
Yash Sharma's act wasn't bad — judges were just very selective in Ep9 given the high bar.
Samay's reaction to the 10 given to Rishikesh & Azad was the most genuine moment of the season.
Amit Tandon gets overlooked but he gave some of the most nuanced feedback of any episode.
Ep9 had the fewest contestants but the highest quality. 3 acts, all strong.
Maheep Singh's energy in Ep9 was chaotic in the best way. He makes the panel livelier.
Rishikesh & MC Azad's 10 should be the gold standard for all future India's Got Latent acts.
Bhavya underscored herself at 9 and still got 9.5. The quiet confidence was beautiful.
Aashish Solanki and Maheep Singh as co-judges had great dynamic — one serious, one chaotic.
Yash Sharma deserved a bit more from the panel. 6 felt low for the effort.
Episode 9 might be the shortest but it was the most emotionally satisfying. That 10 hit different.
Bunti Banerjee getting a clean 9 both ways is the most satisfying score-match of Ep10.
Yashraj as guest judge was entertaining but unpredictable in scoring. Classic rapper energy.
Mysterious Kaiden giving himself a 9 and getting a 9 — who IS this person and where can we find more?
Malay Mudra getting only 5.5 from judges after a 7.5 self-score — the gap is always brutal.
Aashish Solanki stepping in as a replacement panelist in Ep10 was smooth. Seamless integration.
Prateek Vinod is the definition of slightly overconfident. 8.5 self vs 7 judge average.
Yashraj dropping bars between scoring rounds was unscripted gold.
Rahul Singh giving himself 8.5 and getting 6 — the judges were tough this episode.
Bunti Banerjee's 9/9 score match is the kind of validation the show was literally designed to deliver.
Mysterious Kaiden anonymity gimmick actually worked in his favour — full marks for concept AND execution.
Aashish Solanki as replacement judge? Honestly should just be permanent alongside Balraj.
Malay Mudra's act seemed confident but the 5.5 average suggests the judges had seen better.
Yashraj's freestyle commentary while watching acts was genuinely funny and not forced.
Prateek Vinod is proof this format exposes overconfidence better than any other show format.
Samay handling Yashraj's chaotic energy while still running a tight show = elite hosting.
Rahul Singh didn't deserve quite a 6. Felt like judges were calibrating for higher standards.
Bunti Banerjee and Mysterious Kaiden giving Ep10 its backbone. Both delivered.
Aashish Solanki continues to impress every single appearance. Natural fit for this format.
The scoring in Ep10 felt inconsistent for the lower half — but the top acts were great.
Ep10 is elevated by Bunti and Kaiden. Without them it's a weaker episode.
Anmol Sharma and Kushal Bhanushali both getting 9s in the same episode? Ep11 ATE.
GamerFleet / Anshu Bisht as a judge is the most Gen-Z judge choice and I respect the chaos.
Mohammad Adil giving himself 9 and getting 8 — nearly perfect. Solid act.
Nishant Tanwar back for Ep11 — already comfortable in the judge's chair and it shows.
Ankush Langote's 8/8 score is one of the cleanest matches of the season.
Dechen Kirangi Lama giving herself 7.5 and getting 8 — humble and surpassed expectations.
GamerFleet's reactions during acts were more entertaining than some of the acts themselves.
Karan Singh Magic as guest judge was underrated. Brought mentalist-level precision to feedback.
Anmol Sharma's act was the most complete performance of Ep11. The 9 was earned.
Kushal Bhanushali deserved that 9. Style, content, delivery — all there.
Balraj stepping back in as core judge after the Ep9 substitute is reassuring for the format.
GamerFleet judging a live talent show is the most 2024 sentence I've ever read.
Karan Singh Magic's scoring methodology was almost as mysterious as his acts. Intriguing.
Mohammad Adil was one of the most assured performers of the season. That confidence was real.
Dechen Kirangi Lama underselling herself only to outperform is the ideal Latent arc.
Nishant Tanwar is becoming a reliable presence on the show. Growing into the judge role.
Ankush Langote's calm delivery while scoring clean is low-key the most satisfying contestant story.
GamerFleet's scoring was all over the place but his hype energy was great for the room.
Kushal Bhanushali and Anmol Sharma back-to-back 9s in Ep11 is the best consecutive combo of the season.
Episode 11 had the best mid-to-high scoring consistency of any episode. A very satisfying watch.
Khushi Saini getting a 9.5 average while giving herself a 9.5 is a PERFECT score match. Goat behaviour.
Raftaar as a judge was exactly what you'd expect — hyped, sharp, and hilarious.
Yuvraj Gupta's 9 average for a 9 self-score is another perfect match. Ep12 delivered.
Rakhi Sawant walking on for the finale and immediately causing drama is the most Rakhi thing possible.
Sahil Kale as Samay's former manager turned judge is the most wholesome chaotic storyline.
Monal Koli gave herself 8.5 and got 6.5 — respectable gap but the judges were harsh.
Raftaar literally freestyle rapping between scoring sessions is an India's Got Latent special moment.
Rohan getting 8.5 average from a 9 self-score — one of the strongest openers of any episode.
Fitoor giving herself 7.5 and getting only 4.5 — the biggest gap of Ep12 and it was painful.
Sahil Kale judging while clearly knowing Samay too well added a funny meta layer to Ep12.
Khushi Saini's 9.5/9.5 match is the single best score-match of the entire season. Perfect.
Raftaar and Samay together on stage is the collab India didn't know it needed.
Rakhi Sawant's mid-episode walkout drama is chaotic but at least it's never boring.
Fitoor was visibly shocked by the 4.5. The camera work in that moment was storytelling gold.
Yuvraj Gupta's 9/9 accuracy in the finale felt like a full-circle moment for the format.
Nishant Tanwar back for Ep12 as well — he clearly belongs in this format now.
Balraj Singh Ghai holding the finale together as core judge while Rakhi causes chaos = legendary duo.
Rohan deserved a higher score. 8.5 feels like they were calibrating for the bigger finale acts.
Monal Koli had a decent act but the judges in Ep12 had very high standards. Tough room.
Khushi Saini and Yuvraj Gupta closing the season with back-to-back 9s+ is the perfect finale send-off.
